title: Freshly Festive Canadian Peach Skillet
description: A flexitarian-friendly dish featuring peach juice and pickle, perfect for a festive Canadian meal. This malty and springy skillet is sure to delight.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped spring onions and cook until they are softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the cubed tofu and cook until it is lightly browned.
In a small bowl, whisk together the peach juice, malted barley flour, and pickle relish.
Pour the peach juice mixture over the tofu and onions in the skillet.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
